memory.summary: I'm feeling tempted to ditch the project for a quick chore and some leisure, but that procrastination bias is already screaming.

desires: Get that quick chore done so I can feel an instant win and justify ditching the project for a hangout with cohort_3
Avoid looking like the unreliable teammate who flakes on plans right before we meet up
Not feel exhausted by tomorrow night when the real work actually hits

goals.description: Sprint through quick_chore to secure the instant win and moral high ground for ditching the project today.
goals.rationale: My affiliation drive is screaming for time with cohort_3, but my impulsive streak needs a dopamine hit first. Doing this chore now lets me feel justified in skipping the term project entirely today.

goals.description: Lock in plans with cohort_3 immediately so I don't end up flaking at the last minute like an unreliable teammate.
goals.rationale: I value relationships above all else, and being late or ghosting hurts that trust score. However, I'm too excited about the hangout right now to actually schedule it; I'll do it on the way there or right after the chore.

goals.description: Start the actual term project work before 6 PM.
goals.rationale: The deadline isn't until Thursday, and my planning horizon is basically 'now.' Starting this feels like a burden I can push off. If I'm feeling exhausted tomorrow night, that's just bad luck, not my fault.

agent.last_reasoning: I need this instant win to justify ditching the project later. It's too easy to ignore, but finishing it now lets me text cohort_3 immediately after so they don't feel I'm flaking on our plans.
